novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the current flagship is still mainly finite convex selection over an assumed sharp bridge fiber, and needs a stronger generic-class causal obstruction tied directly to named proximal workflows to justify flagship tier.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The §8 why-new block calls the flagship epsilon gap '\overline{S_Q(P)}\subseteq\Theta_I(P)', but that containment is definitional from S_Q(P)⊆Θ_I(P); the stated novelty should be nonempty interior/SPD-exposed coverage/one-dimensional closure equality.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Rewrite Conjecture 1's novelty sentence so the load-bearing claim is the nonempty-interior and one-dimensional closure-equality result, not the trivial subset relation.

Add a precise finite-class reduction showing which Cui OR/IPW/DR or Kallus-Mao-Uehara minimax population objectives correspond to an admissible SPD quadratic selector; otherwise narrow the consumer claim to quadratic residual-penalized bridge solvers.
Promote Conjecture 2 into an explicit matrix witness with T_P, m_P, H_P, ell_P, and the closed-form Q_lambda-selected curve, ruling out engineered or degenerate observable variation.
Replace assumed finite bridge-fiber sharpness with an open-set latent proximal realization theorem, or state a separate conjecture proving sharpness for the same class used in Conjecture 1.

recommended_next_step: Revise the flagship claim to remove the trivial subset phrasing and directly connect the admissible-Q range theorem to named proximal estimator population limits before re-review.
